---
title: "Chapter 18 - Moksha-Sannyasa Yoga"
---

<div class="chapter-title-sanskrit">मोक्षसंन्यासयोगः</div>

<nav class="chapter-nav"><a href="chapter-17.html">Previous chapter</a><span></span></nav>

<div class="verse-list">
<article class="verse" id="v-18-1" data-reference="18.1">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-1">18.1</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">I wish to know, O mighty-armed one, the truth of renunciation, and of relinquishment as well, each apart, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." aria-label="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." title="Hrishikesha: Epithet of Krishna, often understood as lord of the senses." data-glossary-id="hrishikesha">Hrishikesha</span>, slayer of Keshin.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">संन्यासस्य महाबाहो तत्त्वमिच्छामि वेदितुम् ।<br>
त्यागस्य च हृषीकेश पृथक्केशिनिषूदन ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-2" data-reference="18.2">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-2">18.2</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The giving up of desire-driven actions, the sages call renunciation; the surrender of the fruit of all actions, the discerning call relinquishment.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">काम्यानां कर्मणां न्यासं संन्यासं कवयो विदुः ।<br>
सर्वकर्मफलत्यागं प्राहुस्त्यागं विचक्षणाः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-3" data-reference="18.3">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-3">18.3</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Action should be given up as an evil, say some thoughtful men; others say the acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity should not be given up.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">त्याज्यं दोषवदित्येके कर्म प्राहुर्मनीषिणः ।<br>
यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यमिति चापरे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-4" data-reference="18.4">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-4">18.4</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Hear my conclusion on this relinquishment, O best of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharatas</span>; for relinquishment, O tiger among men, is declared to be of three kinds.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">निश्चयं श‍ृणु मे तत्र त्यागे भरतसत्तम ।<br>
त्यागो हि पुरुषव्याघ्र त्रिविधः सम्प्रकीर्तितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-5" data-reference="18.5">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-5">18.5</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Acts of sacrifice, gift, and austerity should not be given up but performed; for sacrifice, gift, and austerity are purifiers of the wise.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यज्ञदानतपःकर्म न त्याज्यं कार्यमेव तत् ।<br>
यज्ञो दानं तपश्चैव पावनानि मनीषिणाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-6" data-reference="18.6">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-6">18.6</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Yet even these actions should be done giving up attachment and their fruits; this, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, is my settled and highest conviction.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च ।<br>
कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्चितं मतमुत्तमम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-7" data-reference="18.7">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-7">18.7</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But the giving up of an enjoined action is not fitting; to abandon it through delusion is held to be <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नियतस्य तु संन्यासः कर्मणो नोपपद्यते ।<br>
मोहात्तस्य परित्यागस्तामसः परिकीर्तितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-8" data-reference="18.8">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-8">18.8</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who gives up an action merely as troublesome, fearing bodily pain, performs a <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span> relinquishment and gains no fruit of it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">दुःखमित्येव यत्कर्म कायक्लेशभयात्त्यजेत् ।<br>
स कृत्वा राजसं त्यागं नैव त्यागफलं लभेत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-9" data-reference="18.9">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-9">18.9</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But when an enjoined action is done simply because it ought to be, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, giving up attachment and fruit—that relinquishment is held <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कार्यमित्येव यत्कर्म नियतं क्रियतेऽर्जुन ।<br>
सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलं चैव स त्यागः सात्त्विको मतः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-10" data-reference="18.10">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-10">18.10</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The relinquisher filled with <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattva</span>, wise and free of doubt, neither shuns disagreeable action nor clings to agreeable action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न द्वेष्ट्यकुशलं कर्म कुशले नानुषज्जते ।<br>
त्यागी सत्त्वसमाविष्टो मेधावी छिन्नसंशयः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-11" data-reference="18.11">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-11">18.11</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For the embodied cannot give up actions entirely; but he who gives up the fruit of action is called a relinquisher.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः ।<br>
यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-12" data-reference="18.12">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-12">18.12</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">For those who do not relinquish, the fruit of action after death is threefold—unwanted, wanted, and mixed; but for those who renounce, there is none at all.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनिष्टमिष्टं मिश्रं च त्रिविधं कर्मणः फलम् ।<br>
भवत्यत्यागिनां प्रेत्य न तु संन्यासिनां क्वचित् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-13" data-reference="18.13">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-13">18.13</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Learn from me, O mighty-armed one, these five factors, declared in the teaching of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." aria-label="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." title="Sankhya: A path or framework of discriminative knowledge; here not always a formal school label." data-glossary-id="sankhya">Sankhya</span> for the accomplishment of all actions:</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पञ्चैतानि महाबाहो कारणानि निबोध मे ।<br>
साङ्ख्ये कृतान्ते प्रोक्तानि सिद्धये सर्वकर्मणाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-14" data-reference="18.14">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-14">18.14</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">the seat of action, the agent, the various instruments, the many and diverse movements, and divine providence as the fifth.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अधिष्ठानं तथा कर्ता करणं च पृथग्विधम् ।<br>
विविधाश्च पृथक्चेष्टा दैवं चैवात्र पञ्चमम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-15" data-reference="18.15">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-15">18.15</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Whatever action a person sets in motion with body, speech, or mind, right or wrong, these five are its factors.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शरीरवाङ्मनोभिर्यत्कर्म प्रारभते नरः ।<br>
न्याय्यं वा विपरीतं वा पञ्चैते तस्य हेतवः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-16" data-reference="18.16">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-16">18.16</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">That being so, the one who, with untrained understanding, sees the self alone as the agent—that fool does not see truly.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तत्रैवं सति कर्तारमात्मानं केवलं तु यः ।<br>
पश्यत्यकृतबुद्धित्वान्न स पश्यति दुर्मतिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-17" data-reference="18.17">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-17">18.17</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who is free of the sense of &#x27;I,&#x27; whose understanding is untainted, though he slay these people, does not slay, and is not bound.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यस्य नाहङ्कृतो भावो बुद्धिर्यस्य न लिप्यते ।<br>
हत्वाऽपि स इमाँल्लोकान्न हन्ति न निबध्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-18" data-reference="18.18">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-18">18.18</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Knowledge, the object of knowledge, and the knower—these are the threefold spur to action; the instrument, the act, and the agent—these are the threefold makeup of action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञानं ज्ञेयं परिज्ञाता त्रिविधा कर्मचोदना ।<br>
करणं कर्म कर्तेति त्रिविधः कर्मसङ्ग्रहः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-19" data-reference="18.19">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-19">18.19</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Knowledge, action, and the agent are said, in the reckoning of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, to be of three kinds, by the difference of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>; hear of these too, as they are.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ज्ञानं कर्म च कर्ता च त्रिधैव गुणभेदतः ।<br>
प्रोच्यते गुणसङ्ख्याने यथावच्छृणु तान्यपि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-20" data-reference="18.20">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-20">18.20</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The knowledge by which one sees in all beings a single, imperishable being, undivided amid the divided—know that knowledge to be <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वभूतेषु येनैकं भावमव्ययमीक्षते ।<br>
अविभक्तं विभक्तेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि सात्त्विकम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-21" data-reference="18.21">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-21">18.21</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But the knowledge that sees in all beings only manifold separate beings, each apart—know that knowledge to be <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">पृथक्त्वेन तु यज्ज्ञानं नानाभावान्पृथग्विधान् ।<br>
वेत्ति सर्वेषु भूतेषु तज्ज्ञानं विद्धि राजसम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-22" data-reference="18.22">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-22">18.22</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But the knowledge that clings to one single thing as if it were the whole, without reason, missing the truth, and narrow—that is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्तु कृत्स्नवदेकस्मिन्कार्ये सक्तमहैतुकम् ।<br>
अतत्त्वार्थवदल्पं च तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-23" data-reference="18.23">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-23">18.23</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Action that is enjoined, done without attachment, without craving or aversion, by one who seeks no fruit, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नियतं सङ्गरहितमरागद्वेषतः कृतम् ।<br>
अफलप्रेप्सुना कर्म यत्तत्सात्त्विकमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-24" data-reference="18.24">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-24">18.24</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But action done with great effort by one seeking to satisfy desire, or out of egotism, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्तु कामेप्सुना कर्म साहङ्कारेण वा पुनः ।<br>
क्रियते बहुलायासं तद्राजसमुदाहृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-25" data-reference="18.25">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-25">18.25</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Action undertaken through delusion, heedless of its consequence, of loss and harm, and of one&#x27;s own power, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अनुबन्धं क्षयं हिंसामनपेक्ष्य च पौरुषम् ।<br>
मोहादारभ्यते कर्म यत्तत्तामसमुच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-26" data-reference="18.26">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-26">18.26</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">An agent free of attachment and ego, possessed of resolve and zeal, unmoved in success and failure, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मुक्तसङ्गोऽनहंवादी धृत्युत्साहसमन्वितः ।<br>
सिद्ध्यसिद्ध्योर्निर्विकारः कर्ता सात्त्विक उच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-27" data-reference="18.27">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-27">18.27</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">An agent driven by passion, craving the fruit of action, greedy, cruel, impure, swept by joy and grief, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः ।<br>
हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-28" data-reference="18.28">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-28">18.28</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">An agent undisciplined, vulgar, stubborn, dishonest, malicious, slothful, despondent, and procrastinating is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अयुक्तः प्राकृतः स्तब्धः शठो नैष्कृतिकोऽलसः ।<br>
विषादी दीर्घसूत्री च कर्ता तामस उच्यते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-29" data-reference="18.29">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-29">18.29</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Now hear, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>, the threefold division of understanding and of resolve, according to the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span>, set out fully and one by one.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बुद्धेर्भेदं धृतेश्चैव गुणतस्त्रिविधं श‍ृणु ।<br>
प्रोच्यमानमशेषेण पृथक्त्वेन धनञ्जय ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-30" data-reference="18.30">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-30">18.30</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The understanding that knows action and renunciation, what is to be done and not done, fear and fearlessness, bondage and liberation, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">प्रवृत्तिं च निवृत्तिं च कार्याकार्ये भयाभये ।<br>
बन्धं मोक्षं च या वेत्ति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-31" data-reference="18.31">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-31">18.31</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The understanding by which one wrongly grasps <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span> and adharma, and what is to be done and not done, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यया धर्ममधर्मं च कार्यं चाकार्यमेव च ।<br>
अयथावत्प्रजानाति बुद्धिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-32" data-reference="18.32">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-32">18.32</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The understanding that, wrapped in darkness, takes adharma to be <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span> and sees all things inverted, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अधर्मं धर्ममिति या मन्यते तमसावृता ।<br>
सर्वार्थान्विपरीतांश्च बुद्धिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-33" data-reference="18.33">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-33">18.33</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The resolve by which one holds steady the workings of the mind, the breath, and the senses, through unwavering <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>—that resolve, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">धृत्या यया धारयते मनःप्राणेन्द्रियक्रियाः ।<br>
योगेनाव्यभिचारिण्या धृतिः सा पार्थ सात्त्विकी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-34" data-reference="18.34">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-34">18.34</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">But the resolve by which one clings, with attachment to fruit, to <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, pleasure, and wealth, longing for their reward, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यया तु धर्मकामार्थान्धृत्या धारयतेऽर्जुन ।<br>
प्रसङ्गेन फलाकाङ्क्षी धृतिः सा पार्थ राजसी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-35" data-reference="18.35">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-35">18.35</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The resolve by which the dull-witted will not let go of sleep, fear, grief, despair, and folly—that resolve, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, is <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यया स्वप्नं भयं शोकं विषादं मदमेव च ।<br>
न विमुञ्चति दुर्मेधा धृतिः सा पार्थ तामसी ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-36" data-reference="18.36">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-36">18.36</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Now hear from me, O best of the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharatas</span>, the threefold happiness, that in which one comes to delight through practice and reaches the end of suffering.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सुखं त्विदानीं त्रिविधं श‍ृणु मे भरतर्षभ ।<br>
अभ्यासाद्रमते यत्र दुःखान्तं च निगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-37" data-reference="18.37">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-37">18.37</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The happiness that is like poison at first but like nectar in the end, born of the serene clarity of one&#x27;s own understanding, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." aria-label="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." title="sattva: The guna associated with clarity, balance, and illumination." data-glossary-id="sattva">sattvic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्तदग्रे विषमिव परिणामेऽमृतोपमम् ।<br>
तत्सुखं सात्त्विकं प्रोक्तमात्मबुद्धिप्रसादजम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-38" data-reference="18.38">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-38">18.38</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The happiness born of the contact of the senses with their objects, like nectar at first but like poison in the end, is held to be <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." aria-label="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." title="rajas: The guna associated with activity, passion, impulse, and restlessness." data-glossary-id="rajas">rajasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">विषयेन्द्रियसंयोगाद्यत्तदग्रेऽमृतोपमम् ।<br>
परिणामे विषमिव तत्सुखं राजसं स्मृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-39" data-reference="18.39">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-39">18.39</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The happiness that deludes the self both at first and in the end, arising from sleep, sloth, and heedlessness, is called <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." aria-label="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." title="tamas: The guna associated with inertia, dullness, confusion, and obstruction." data-glossary-id="tamas">tamasic</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदग्रे चानुबन्धे च सुखं मोहनमात्मनः ।<br>
निद्रालस्यप्रमादोत्थं तत्तामसमुदाहृतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-40" data-reference="18.40">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-40">18.40</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">There is no being, on earth or among the gods in heaven, that is free of these three <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span> born of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." aria-label="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." title="prakriti: Nature, material process, or the field of change and manifestation." data-glossary-id="prakriti">prakriti</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न तदस्ति पृथिव्यां वा दिवि देवेषु वा पुनः ।<br>
सत्त्वं प्रकृतिजैर्मुक्तं यदेभिः स्यात्त्रिभिर्गुणैः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-41" data-reference="18.41">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-41">18.41</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The duties of brahmins, kshatriyas, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="vaishya: Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding." aria-label="vaishya: Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding." title="vaishya: Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding." data-glossary-id="vaishya">vaishyas</span>, and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="shudra: Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme." aria-label="shudra: Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme." title="shudra: Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme." data-glossary-id="shudra">shudras</span>, O scorcher of foes, are apportioned by the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." aria-label="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." title="guna: A quality or constituent tendency of prakriti: sattva, rajas, and tamas." data-glossary-id="guna">gunas</span> that arise from their own natures.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ब्राह्मणक्षत्रियविशां शूद्राणां च परन्तप ।<br>
कर्माणि प्रविभक्तानि स्वभावप्रभवैर्गुणैः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-42" data-reference="18.42">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-42">18.42</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Calm, restraint, austerity, purity, patience, and uprightness; knowledge, realization, and faith in the unseen—these are the duty of a <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." aria-label="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." title="Brahmin: A member of the priestly or learned class; Sanskrit brāhmaṇa." data-glossary-id="brahmin">brahmin</span>, born of his nature.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शमो दमस्तपः शौचं क्षान्तिरार्जवमेव च ।<br>
ज्ञानं विज्ञानमास्तिक्यं ब्रह्मकर्म स्वभावजम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-43" data-reference="18.43">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-43">18.43</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Valor, vigor, fortitude, skill, not fleeing in battle, generosity, and lordly bearing—these are the duty of a <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="kshatriya: Member of the warrior or ruling class." aria-label="kshatriya: Member of the warrior or ruling class." title="kshatriya: Member of the warrior or ruling class." data-glossary-id="kshatriya">kshatriya</span>, born of his nature.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">शौर्यं तेजो धृतिर्दाक्ष्यं युद्धे चाप्यपलायनम् ।<br>
दानमीश्वरभावश्च क्षात्रं कर्म स्वभावजम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-44" data-reference="18.44">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-44">18.44</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Farming, herding, and trade are the duty of a <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="vaishya: Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding." aria-label="vaishya: Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding." title="vaishya: Member of the class associated with trade, agriculture, and herding." data-glossary-id="vaishya">vaishya</span>, born of his nature; and service is the duty of a <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="shudra: Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme." aria-label="shudra: Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme." title="shudra: Member of the service class in the traditional varna scheme." data-glossary-id="shudra">shudra</span>, born of his nature.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कृषिगौरक्ष्यवाणिज्यं वैश्यकर्म स्वभावजम् ।<br>
परिचर्यात्मकं कर्म शूद्रस्यापि स्वभावजम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-45" data-reference="18.45">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-45">18.45</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Devoted each to his own work, a person attains perfection; hear how one devoted to his own work finds it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स्वे स्वे कर्मण्यभिरतः संसिद्धिं लभते नरः ।<br>
स्वकर्मनिरतः सिद्धिं यथा विन्दति तच्छृणु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-46" data-reference="18.46">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-46">18.46</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He from whom all beings spring, and by whom all this is pervaded—worshiping him through his own work, a person attains perfection.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यतः प्रवृत्तिर्भूतानां येन सर्वमिदं ततम् ।<br>
स्वकर्मणा तमभ्यर्च्य सिद्धिं विन्दति मानवः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-47" data-reference="18.47">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-47">18.47</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Better one&#x27;s own <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>, imperfectly done, than another&#x27;s <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span> well performed; doing the work set by one&#x27;s own nature, one incurs no fault.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">श्रेयान्स्वधर्मो विगुणः परधर्मात्स्वनुष्ठितात् ।<br>
स्वभावनियतं कर्म कुर्वन्नाप्नोति किल्बिषम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-48" data-reference="18.48">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-48">18.48</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">One should not give up the work born of one&#x27;s nature, O son of Kunti, even if it is flawed; for all undertakings are wrapped in flaw, as fire in smoke.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सहजं कर्म कौन्तेय सदोषमपि न त्यजेत् ।<br>
सर्वारम्भा हि दोषेण धूमेनाग्निरिवावृताः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-49" data-reference="18.49">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-49">18.49</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He whose understanding is unattached everywhere, his self subdued, his longing gone, attains through renunciation the supreme perfection of freedom from action.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">असक्तबुद्धिः सर्वत्र जितात्मा विगतस्पृहः ।<br>
नैष्कर्म्यसिद्धिं परमां संन्यासेनाधिगच्छति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-50" data-reference="18.50">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-50">18.50</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Hear from me in brief, O son of Kunti, how one who has attained perfection attains <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span> as well—that highest consummation of knowledge.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सिद्धिं प्राप्तो यथा ब्रह्म तथाप्नोति निबोध मे ।<br>
समासेनैव कौन्तेय निष्ठा ज्ञानस्य या परा ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-51" data-reference="18.51">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-51">18.51</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Yoked with a purified understanding, restraining the self with firmness, turning away from sound and the other objects of sense, casting off craving and aversion,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">बुद्ध्या विशुद्धया युक्तो धृत्यात्मानं नियम्य च ।<br>
शब्दादीन्विषयांस्त्यक्त्वा रागद्वेषौ व्युदस्य च ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-52" data-reference="18.52">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-52">18.52</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">dwelling apart, eating little, his speech, body, and mind controlled, ever absorbed in the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of meditation, taking refuge in dispassion,</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">विविक्तसेवी लघ्वाशी यतवाक्कायमानसः ।<br>
ध्यानयोगपरो नित्यं वैराग्यं समुपाश्रितः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-53" data-reference="18.53">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-53">18.53</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">freed from ego, force, pride, desire, anger, and possession, without a sense of &#x27;mine,&#x27; and at peace—he is fit for the state of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अहङ्कारं बलं दर्पं कामं क्रोधं परिग्रहम् ।<br>
विमुच्य निर्ममः शान्तो ब्रह्मभूयाय कल्पते ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-54" data-reference="18.54">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-54">18.54</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Become <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." aria-label="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." title="Brahman: The ultimate reality or absolute principle." data-glossary-id="brahman">Brahman</span>, serene in self, he neither grieves nor craves; the same toward all beings, he attains supreme devotion to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ब्रह्मभूतः प्रसन्नात्मा न शोचति न काङ्क्षति ।<br>
समः सर्वेषु भूतेषु मद्भक्तिं लभते पराम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-55" data-reference="18.55">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-55">18.55</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">By devotion he comes to know me, who and how great I truly am; and knowing me in truth, he enters into me forthwith.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">भक्त्या मामभिजानाति यावान्यश्चास्मि तत्त्वतः ।<br>
ततो मां तत्त्वतो ज्ञात्वा विशते तदनन्तरम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-56" data-reference="18.56">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-56">18.56</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Ever performing all actions, taking refuge in me, by my grace he reaches the eternal, imperishable abode.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वकर्माण्यपि सदा कुर्वाणो मद्व्यपाश्रयः ।<br>
मत्प्रसादादवाप्नोति शाश्वतं पदमव्ययम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-57" data-reference="18.57">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-57">18.57</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Surrendering all actions to me in your thought, intent on me, taking refuge in the <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> of understanding, keep your mind ever fixed on me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">चेतसा सर्वकर्माणि मयि संन्यस्य मत्परः ।<br>
बुद्धियोगमुपाश्रित्य मच्चित्तः सततं भव ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-58" data-reference="18.58">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-58">18.58</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">With your mind on me, you will cross over all difficulties by my grace; but if through ego you will not listen, you will be lost.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मच्चित्तः सर्वदुर्गाणि मत्प्रसादात्तरिष्यसि ।<br>
अथ चेत्त्वमहङ्कारान्न श्रोष्यसि विनङ्क्ष्यसि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-59" data-reference="18.59">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-59">18.59</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">If, relying on your ego, you think, &#x27;I will not fight,&#x27; vain is this resolve of yours; your own nature will compel you.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यदहङ्कारमाश्रित्य न योत्स्य इति मन्यसे ।<br>
मिथ्यैष व्यवसायस्ते प्रकृतिस्त्वां नियोक्ष्यति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-60" data-reference="18.60">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-60">18.60</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Bound by your own work, born of your nature, O son of Kunti, what through delusion you wish not to do, you will do even against your will.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">स्वभावजेन कौन्तेय निबद्धः स्वेन कर्मणा ।<br>
कर्तुं नेच्छसि यन्मोहात्करिष्यस्यवशोऽपि तत् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-61" data-reference="18.61">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-61">18.61</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">The lord dwells in the heart of all beings, <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, whirling them all about by his <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." aria-label="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." title="maya: Power of appearance, concealment, or manifestation; context-dependent." data-glossary-id="maya">maya</span>, as though mounted on a machine.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">ईश्वरः सर्वभूतानां हृद्देशेऽर्जुन तिष्ठति ।<br>
भ्रामयन्सर्वभूतानि यन्त्रारूढानि मायया ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-62" data-reference="18.62">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-62">18.62</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Take refuge in him alone with all your being, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." aria-label="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." title="Bharata: A dynastic or ancestral name, often used as an address." data-glossary-id="bharata">Bharata</span>; by his grace you will attain supreme peace and the eternal abode.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तमेव शरणं गच्छ सर्वभावेन भारत ।<br>
तत्प्रसादात्परां शान्तिं स्थानं प्राप्स्यसि शाश्वतम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-63" data-reference="18.63">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-63">18.63</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus I have declared to you the knowledge more secret than all secrets; ponder it fully, and then do as you wish.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इति ते ज्ञानमाख्यातं गुह्याद्गुह्यतरं मया ।<br>
विमृश्यैतदशेषेण यथेच्छसि तथा कुरु ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-64" data-reference="18.64">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-64">18.64</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Hear again my supreme word, the most secret of all; you are greatly loved by me, and so I shall tell you what is for your good.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वगुह्यतमं भूयः श‍ृणु मे परमं वचः ।<br>
इष्टोऽसि मे दृढमिति ततो वक्ष्यामि ते हितम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-65" data-reference="18.65">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-65">18.65</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Fix your mind on me, be devoted to me, sacrifice to me, bow before me; so you will come to me. I promise you truly, for you are dear to me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">मन्मना भव मद्भक्तो मद्याजी मां नमस्कुरु ।<br>
मामेवैष्यसि सत्यं ते प्रतिजाने प्रियोऽसि मे ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-66" data-reference="18.66">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-66">18.66</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Giving up all dharmas, take refuge in me alone; I will free you from all sins—do not grieve.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज ।<br>
अहं त्वा सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-67" data-reference="18.67">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-67">18.67</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">This is never to be told by you to one without austerity or devotion, to one who will not listen, or to one who scorns me.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इदं ते नातपस्काय नाभक्ताय कदाचन ।<br>
न चाशुश्रूषवे वाच्यं न च मां योऽभ्यसूयति ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-68" data-reference="18.68">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-68">18.68</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">He who shall declare this supreme secret among those devoted to me, offering me the highest devotion, will come to me beyond doubt.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">य इदं परमं गुह्यं मद्भक्तेष्वभिधास्यति ।<br>
भक्तिं मयि परां कृत्वा मामेवैष्यत्यसंशयः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-69" data-reference="18.69">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-69">18.69</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Among men there is none who does me dearer service, and there will be no one on earth dearer to me than he.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">न च तस्मान्मनुष्येषु कश्चिन्मे प्रियकृत्तमः ।<br>
भविता न च मे तस्मादन्यः प्रियतरो भुवि ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-70" data-reference="18.70">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-70">18.70</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And he who studies this our dialogue on <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." aria-label="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." title="dharma: Order, duty, law, or right conduct; the exact sense depends on context." data-glossary-id="dharma">dharma</span>—by him I shall be worshiped with the sacrifice of knowledge; so I hold.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">अध्येष्यते च य इमं धर्म्यं संवादमावयोः ।<br>
ज्ञानयज्ञेन तेनाहमिष्टः स्यामिति मे मतिः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-71" data-reference="18.71">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-71">18.71</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And the one who merely hears it, full of faith and free of scorn—he too, set free, will attain the happy worlds of those who do right.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">श्रद्धावाननसूयश्च श‍ृणुयादपि यो नरः ।<br>
सोऽपि मुक्तः शुभाँल्लोकान्प्राप्नुयात्पुण्यकर्मणाम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-72" data-reference="18.72">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-72">18.72</a>
<span class="speaker">(Krishna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Have you heard this, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, with mind one-pointed? Has your delusion born of ignorance been destroyed, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." aria-label="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." title="Dhananjaya: Epithet of Arjuna, often understood as winner of wealth." data-glossary-id="dhananjaya">Dhananjaya</span>?</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">कच्चिदेतच्छ्रुतं पार्थ त्वयैकाग्रेण चेतसा ।<br>
कच्चिदज्ञानसम्मोहः प्रनष्टस्ते धनञ्जय ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-73" data-reference="18.73">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-73">18.73</a>
<span class="speaker">(Arjuna)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">My delusion is gone; by your grace, O <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Acyuta: Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one." aria-label="Acyuta: Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one." title="Acyuta: Epithet of Krishna, often glossed as the unfailing one." data-glossary-id="acyuta">Acyuta</span>, I have regained my memory. I stand firm, my doubts dispelled; I will do your word.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">नष्टो मोहः स्मृतिर्लब्धा त्वत्प्रसादान्मयाच्युत ।<br>
स्थितोऽस्मि गतसन्देहः करिष्ये वचनं तव ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-74" data-reference="18.74">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-74">18.74</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Thus have I heard this wondrous dialogue between <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." aria-label="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." title="Vasudeva: Name or epithet of Krishna, son of Vasudeva." data-glossary-id="vasudeva">Vasudeva</span> and the great-souled <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, making my hair stand on end.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">इत्यहं वासुदेवस्य पार्थस्य च महात्मनः ।<br>
संवादमिममश्रौषमद्भुतं रोमहर्षणम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-75" data-reference="18.75">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-75">18.75</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">By the grace of Vyasa I have heard this supreme and most secret <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span> directly from <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>, the lord of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, as he himself declared it.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">व्यासप्रसादाच्छ्रुतवानेतद्गुह्यमहं परम् ।<br>
योगं योगेश्वरात्कृष्णात्साक्षात्कथयतः स्वयम् ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-76" data-reference="18.76">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-76">18.76</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">O king, recalling again and again this wondrous and holy dialogue of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." aria-label="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." title="Keshava: Epithet of Krishna." data-glossary-id="keshava">Keshava</span> and <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." aria-label="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." title="Arjuna: Arjuna, the warrior addressed by Krishna." data-glossary-id="arjuna">Arjuna</span>, I rejoice again and again.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">राजन्संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य संवादमिममद्भुतम् ।<br>
केशवार्जुनयोः पुण्यं हृष्यामि च मुहुर्मुहुः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-77" data-reference="18.77">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-77">18.77</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">And recalling again and again that most wondrous form of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Hari: Epithet of Krishna or Vishnu." aria-label="Hari: Epithet of Krishna or Vishnu." title="Hari: Epithet of Krishna or Vishnu." data-glossary-id="hari">Hari</span>, great is my wonder, O king, and I rejoice again and again.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">तच्च संस्मृत्य संस्मृत्य रूपमत्यद्भुतं हरेः ।<br>
विस्मयो मे महान् राजन्हृष्यामि च पुनः पुनः ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

<article class="verse" id="v-18-78" data-reference="18.78">
<header class="verse-head">
<a class="ref" href="#v-18-78">18.78</a>
<span class="speaker">(Sanjaya)</span>
</header>
<p class="translation translation-en" data-lang="en">Wherever <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." aria-label="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." title="Krishna: Krishna, speaker of the teaching to Arjuna." data-glossary-id="krishna">Krishna</span>, the lord of <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." aria-label="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." title="yoga: Discipline, practice, integration, or path toward steadiness and liberation. Literal meaning is &quot;yoking&quot;, &quot;uniting&quot;, &quot;joining&quot; (from the root yuj: &quot;to yoke&quot;, &quot;to unite&quot;)." data-glossary-id="yoga">yoga</span>, is, and wherever <span class="glossary-term" tabindex="0" data-gloss="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." aria-label="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." title="Partha: Epithet of Arjuna: son of Pritha/Kunti." data-glossary-id="partha">Partha</span>, the archer, is, there, I hold, are fortune, victory, well-being, and steadfast right conduct.</p>
<details class="sanskrit">
<summary>Sanskrit</summary>
<div class="sanskrit-text">यत्र योगेश्वरः कृष्णो यत्र पार्थो धनुर्धरः ।<br>
तत्र श्रीर्विजयो भूतिर्ध्रुवा नीतिर्मतिर्मम ॥</div>
</details>
</article>

</div>
